data.title: Wells Fargo in charts: Q1 Consumer Banking and Lending revenue down Y/Y
Driven Brands makes the strategic decision to sell its U.S. car wash business
data.description:
Driven Brands Holdings (NASDAQ:DRVN) announced that it closed on the sale of its U.S. car wash business to Express Wash Operations.

Under the terms of the agreement, Whistle paid Driven Brands (NASDAQ:DRVN) approximately $255 million in cash and delivered a negotiable interest-bearing seller note in the principal amount of $130 million. Final proceeds are subject to customary post-closing adjustments and payment of transaction-related fees and expenses. 

Driven Brands (DRVN) said cash proceeds from the transaction will be used to pay down debt as it works to achieve 3X or less net leverage by the end of 2026. In FY25, Driven Brands (DRVN) plans to report the results of the U.S. car wash business as discontinued operations.

Looking ahead, the Charlotte-based company expects the asset sale to mark an important milestone by enabling it to focus on the Take 5 Oil Change business. CEO Jonathan Fitzpatrick noted that Driven Brands'(DRVN) is now underpinned by stable, cash-generating franchise businesses. "We are confident this disposition will better position the company to execute on our strategic initiatives," he noted.

In FY25, Driven Brands (DRVN) plans to report the results of the U.S. car wash business as discontinued operations.

data.title: Fastenal GAAP EPS of $0.52 in-line, revenue of $1.96B beats by $10M
data.description:
• Fastenal press release (NASDAQ:FAST): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.52 in-line.
  • Revenue of $1.96B (+3.7% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • Net sales increased $64.3, or 3.4%, in the first quarter of 2025 when compared to the first quarter of 2024. There was one less selling day in the first quarter of 2025 relative to the prior year period and, taking this into consideration, our net daily sales increased 5.0% in the first quarter of 2025 compared to the first quarter of 2024.

data.title: JD.com to set up $27B fund to boost exporters domestic sales as trade war heats up - report
data.description:
China's JD.com (NASDAQ:JD) will reportedly spend $27.B to set up a fund to boost the country's exporters to sell their products in the domestic market over the next year as the U.S.-China trade war heats up. 

China has announced an increase in tariffs on U.S. goods to 125%, up from the previous rate of 84%, effective April 12, and stated it will disregard any additional tariff hikes announced by Washington moving forward.

The company will send its staff to Chinese companies that deal in foreign trade and directly buy their "high quality products" and will look to set up an area on its e-commerce site to sell these products, Reuters reported.

Amidst U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs, Chinese exporters are expected to see losses, however, programs aimed at helping the exporters could mitigate some of the hit from reduced sales. 

 The White House on Thursday clarified that the cumulative tariff on China-produced goods coming into the U.S. will total 145%.

Trump's tariff announcements have put pressure on JD.com, dragging its shares down nearly 13% over the past month.

data.title: Wells Fargo Non-GAAP EPS of $1.27 beats by $0.05, revenue of $20.15B misses by $610M
data.description:
• Wells Fargo press release (NYSE:WFC): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.27 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $20.15B (-3.4% Y/Y) misses by $610M.
  • Net interest income decreased 6%, driven by the impact of lower interest rates on floating rate assets, deposit mix and pricing changes, lower loan balances, and one fewer day in the quarter, partially offset by lower market funding
  • Provision for credit losses of $932M included a decrease in the allowance for credit losses, reflecting a lower allowance for commercial real estate loans on lower loan balances, partially offset by a higher allowance for commercial and industrial loans

data.title: JPMorgan Non-GAAP EPS of $4.91 beats by $0.27, revenue of $46B beats by $1.86B
data.description:
• JPMorgan press release (NYSE:JPM): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $4.91 beats by $0.27.
  • Revenue of $46B (+9.7% Y/Y) beats by $1.86B.
  •  Book value per share of $119.24, up 12% YoY; tangible book value per share2 of $100.36, up 13% YoY
  • Basel III common equity Tier 1 capital of $280 billion, Standardized ratio of 15.4% and Advanced ratio of 15.5%
  • Assets under management were $4.1 trillion, and client assets were $6.0 trillion, each up 15%, driven by continued net inflows and higher market levels.
  • The provision for credit losses was $3.3 billion. Net charge-offs were $2.3 billion, up $376 million, predominantly driven by Card Services. The net reserve build of $973 million included $549 million in Wholesale and $441 million in Consumer and was largely driven by changes in the weighted-average macroeconomic outlook. The prior-year provision was $1.9 billion, net charge-offs were $2.0 billion and the net reserve release was $72 million.
  • Shares +2%.

data.title: Topgolf Callaway attracts praise from analysts after Jack Wolfskin deal
data.description:
Topgolf Callaway Brands (NYSE:MODG) is being sized up after the company announced this week that it has entered into an agreement to sell its Jack Wolfskin business to ANTA Sports for a base price of $290 million in cash. The deal is expected to close late in Q2 or early in Q3.

Topgolf Callaway CEO Chip Brewer said the sale will allow the company to increase focus and optimize resources on its core business. "Importantly, the proceeds will further enhance our balance sheet and liquidity, reinforcing our financial flexibility ahead of our planned separation of Topgolf from our core operations," he highlighted.

Roth Capital analyst George Kelly said the deal value was greater than the firm anticipated and will help MODG "de-lever " ahead of the planned Topgolf separation. Kelly and his team thought the Jack Wolfskin brand was always a poor fit and distraction for Topgolf Callaway (NYSE:MODG).

In terms of Topgolf Callaway's (MODG) balance sheet, Roth Capital sees potential for Topgolf to exit with zero debt on a REIT-adjusted basis and for the legacy business to carry less than 3X net leverage, without factoring in a worse tariff situation.

Roth Capital maintained a Buy rating on Topgolf Callaway's (MODG) and assigned a price target of $11 (+80% upside) based on a sum-of-the-parts analysis that has strengthened following the Jack announcement.

Jefferies also views the Jack Wolfskin sale as a step in the right direction for Topgolf Callaway Brands (MODG).  Analyst Randal Konik said the brand has faced challenges with declining sales in Europe, and revenue is projected to decrease further in FY25. "With the Topgolf spin-off expected to be completed later this year, we see strong value in MODG shares, which we believe have found a bottom," he highlighted on the Buy-rated stock.

On Seeking Alpha, Investing Group Leader Daniel Jones called MODG significantly undervalued on Friday and issued a Strong Buy rating.

Shares of Topgolf Callaway (MODG) are down 20.2% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Spotlight on pet stocks on National Pet Day
data.description:
April 11 is National Pet Day in the United States. The holiday has seen an increase in promotions from retailers over the last few years, including deals from Amazon (AMZN), Petco (WOOF), PetSmart, Chewy (CHWY) and independent pet stores.  

The pet industry is still considered to be in a growth phase, despite some concerns about a post-pandemic letdown. Seven out of 10 U.S. households currently have pets, which is more than have children. Owners are providing pets with premium foods, luxury services, state-of-the-art health care, insurance policies and more. Millennials are the largest group of pet owners and spend more annually on pet care compared to Gen Z.

Since 2013, pet spending has risen by 90%, reflecting both the growing pet population and the expanding range of products and services available for pets.

Looking ahead, the global pet care industry could exceed $350 billion by 2027. Domestically, it has grown steadily every year since 2001.

For investors, a catch-all for the pet industry is the Pet Care ETF (BATS:PAWZ), which has outperformed the FactSet Pet Care Index in 2025. Holdings in the ETF include Chewy (CHWY), Idexx Laboratories (IDXX), Zoetis (ZTS), Freshpet (FPRT), Virbac SA (OTCPK:VRBCF), Trupanion (TRUP), Nestle (OTCPK:NSRGY), CVS Health (CVS), Petco Health & Wellness (WOOF), Central Garden & Pet (CENT), Colgate-Palmolive (CL), and General Mills (GIS).

data.title: Stock index futures rise, yields mixed even as China slaps 125% tariffs on U.S. goods
data.description:
Stock index futures rose sharply on Friday, even as the U.S.-China trade war seemed to heat up, with China further retaliating against President Donald Trump's tariffs.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.7%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.9%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.6%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.42%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 3 basis points to 3.81%.

China has announced an increase in tariffs on U.S. goods to 125%, up from the previous rate of 84%, effective April 12, and stated it will disregard any additional tariff hikes announced by Washington moving forward.

Wall Street's euphoria disappeared on Thursday as market participants were back in selloff mode.

"The main driver of the renewed market pressure was an increased focus on the US-China escalation, with yesterday’s market downturn accelerating after a White House clarification that total tariffs on China would now be 145% rather than 125%," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said.

While this difference is negligible in any practical economic sense, the market reaction showed an increased sensitivity to the risks of a disorderly economic decoupling between the world’s two largest economies that we highlighted yesterday, Sidorov added.

Trump’s administration clarified the 125% tax on imports from China was 145%. This increase will not shift demand patterns. For investors, this raises policy competence questions again. A well-planned trade tax would consider costs. Tax rates set seemingly at random question whether costs are properly considered, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessent said that they saw nothing unusual in yesterday’s market moves. The 30-year Treasury yield experienced the biggest increase since 1982, equities are falling, the dollar is falling, and gold hit an all-time high. Economists might consider this pattern not entirely usual," Donovan added. 

On the economic side, the March wholesale inflation data will land before the bell. Core PPI is expected to come in at 0.3% M/M and 3.6% Y/Y.

PPI is expected to come in at 3.3% Y/Y and 0.2% M/M.    

The March consumer sentiment report will land later in the day and is forecasted to come in at 54.

Traders will also keep a close eye on the banks' Q1 earnings starting on Friday.
